audit_items.proof_trace: The REWRITE_ACTION_USER branch at line 369 dereferences action->la_args without any null check or assert, while the analogous rewrite_action_goto path (also in context.c) explicitly asserts action->la_args != NULL before dereference (line 173). Normal construction in rewrite_rule_compile always sets la_args before creating REWRITE_ACTION_USER actions, so current code paths are safe; however, destroy_action in rule.c uses incorrect switch constants (REWRITE_FLAG_GOTO/'G'=71 vs REWRITE_ACTION_GOTO=3) that never match, revealing inconsistent invariant assumptions about la_args across the codebase — the defensive null check never executes and la_args is always leaked.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An LDAP rewrite configuration with a 'U' (user-error) flag on a rule, combined with any code path that creates a REWRITE_ACTION_USER action without setting la_args (e.g., programmatic API misuse or a future code path), would cause a null-pointer dereference crash in rewrite_context_apply when an LDAP client sends a request matching that rule, enabling remote denial of service.
